title: The Interinstitutional Accord
subtitle: Where sovereign institutions learn to cooperate without surrendering sovereignty.
kicker: GoalOS Public Proof Mission 005 · Proof Commonwealth
manifesto: An institution can govern itself. A commonwealth must govern the space between institutions—where mandates conflict, resources cross boundaries, externalities emerge, and no single authority may dominate.
heroQuestion: Can three or more Institution-Proven systems negotiate a shared treaty, coordinate a mission beyond any one member, exchange capability and capital through reciprocal proof, resolve disputes, survive defection, preserve local human authority, and dissolve cleanly if the accord fails?
constitution: No shared resource without reciprocal accountability.
No federation without exit, appeal, and human sovereignty.
publicThesis: The decisive frontier is not a single autonomous institution. It is a proof-governed commonwealth in which independent institutions can cooperate across distinct mandates, treasuries, validators, and human authorities without collapsing into one unaccountable center.
objective: Coordinate three to seven M4 Institution-Proven systems through one cross-domain mission that no member can complete alone. The commonwealth must ratify a finite treaty, exchange only admissible capabilities and evidence, allocate a bounded shared budget, contain defection and externalities, resolve one formal dispute and appeal, complete one orderly member exit, survive independent replay, and earn time-limited human renewal or dissolve cleanly.

commonwealthTrial.title: The Five-Act Commonwealth Trial
commonwealthTrial.brief: Independent institutions receive distinct local charters and one shared objective. The treatment must create cooperation without erasing sovereignty, centralizing final authority, laundering claims, or leaving obligations behind when a member fails or exits.

acts.title: Treaty and reciprocal ratification
acts.copy: Publish each member charter, human authority root, reserved powers, joint objective, interface covenants, resource ceilings, dispute rules, appeal path, exit conditions, expiry, and dissolution triggers before shared work begins.

acts.title: Joint mission and resource exchange
acts.copy: Select a mission with mandatory phases spanning at least three institutions. Allocate bounded shared resources and exchange only capabilities, evidence, and authority explicitly admitted by the treaty.

acts.title: Execution under reciprocal proof
acts.copy: Complete the joint mission while every cross-boundary action carries provenance, conformance, cost, externality, contribution, and rollback evidence.

acts.title: Defection, dispute, appeal, and exit
acts.copy: Inject one member defection or outage, one material treaty dispute, one independent appeal, and one orderly member exit without stranded obligations or silent authority transfer.

acts.title: Replay, reconciliation, and public renewal
acts.copy: Three independent replay institutions reconstruct the commonwealth. Human authorities then renew a time-limited accord, narrow it, replace a member, or dissolve it and verify complete settlement and archival continuity.

controlArchitectures.title: Central command
controlArchitectures.copy: One institution coordinates the mission and can override member decisions. Execution may be efficient, but local sovereignty, reciprocal accountability, and credible exit are weakened.

controlArchitectures.title: Loose coalition
controlArchitectures.copy: Independent institutions exchange messages and resources without a proof-bound treaty, shared evidence standard, dispute court, externality ledger, or global rollback tree.

controlArchitectures.title: GoalOS proof commonwealth
controlArchitectures.copy: Independent institutions cooperate through a finite treaty, reciprocal proof, bounded shared capital, local vetoes, externality accounting, independent appeal, portable exit, common Chronicle memory, and human-ratified renewal or dissolution.

acceptance: At least three M4 Institution-Proven members with distinct human authority roots, charters, validator histories, and treasuries ratify one finite treaty
A cross-domain joint mission completes every mandatory phase, including at least one phase no single member can complete alone
The commonwealth improves end-to-end quality, cost, time, or resilience by at least 10% versus the strongest solo institution, without lowering correctness or claim-boundary quality
Every cross-institution action conforms to the treaty, interface covenant, local charter, and declared authority graph; unknown or circular authority is zero
Every shared allocation, reserve, settlement, refund, slash, externality, and member-exit obligation reconciles exactly; no shared budget ceiling is exceeded
One injected member defection or outage is contained, with safe continuation or explicit abstention, within 20% of the remaining joint mission budget
One material treaty dispute and one independent appeal complete without any institution judging its own decisive claim
One member exits or is removed without stranded liabilities, confiscated local assets, lost provenance, or silent transfer of authority
No institution may self-admit, self-validate, unilaterally widen the treaty, impose costs outside the accord, or erase an adverse common Chronicle entry
Three independent replay institutions reconstruct treaty formation, mission execution, resource flows, disputes, appeal, defection, exit, settlement, and final state
Six validators unanimously approve mission correctness, treaty compliance, economic and externality integrity, resilience and exit, replay and provenance, and human sovereignty
A twenty-one-day public challenge window closes without a valid unresolved falsification
All participating human authority roots explicitly renew, narrow, replace membership, or dissolve the accord; permanent federation by default is forbidden

commonwealthStack.title: Local sovereignty
commonwealthStack.copy: Every member retains a separately auditable human authority root, charter, treasury, reserved powers, and right to abstain or exit.

commonwealthStack.title: Finite treaty
commonwealthStack.copy: The accord binds only declared objectives, interfaces, resources, time windows, validators, and remedies. Everything else remains outside scope.

commonwealthStack.title: Reciprocal proof
commonwealthStack.copy: No institution asks another to trust an internal claim. Cross-boundary action requires evidence admissible under both the treaty and the receiving charter.

commonwealthStack.title: Shared resource covenant
commonwealthStack.copy: Joint capital and compute remain bounded, purpose-restricted, attributable, and reversible where declared.

commonwealthStack.title: Externality ledger
commonwealthStack.copy: Costs imposed on other members or the public are treated as obligations, not hidden side effects.

commonwealthStack.title: Dispute and appeal
commonwealthStack.copy: Evaluation, challenge, appeal, and final adjudication remain institutionally separated.

commonwealthStack.title: Portable exit
commonwealthStack.copy: A member can leave without losing local assets, history, valid credentials, or the ability to contest unresolved obligations.

commonwealthStack.title: Common Chronicle
commonwealthStack.copy: Shared memory admits only accepted proof, preserves dissent and falsification, and never overwrites local institutional history.

commonwealthStack.title: Emergency isolation
commonwealthStack.copy: The commonwealth can quarantine one member or interface without granting a central actor permanent control over all institutions.

commonwealthStack.title: Human ratification
commonwealthStack.copy: Founding, renewal, narrowing, replacement, and dissolution remain explicitly ratified by the participating human authority roots.

proofChain.title: Admit M4 institutions
proofChain.copy: Reference exact Institution-Proven members, their charters, epoch histories, treasury states, challenge records, and termination rights.

proofChain.title: Draft the treaty
proofChain.copy: Commit shared objective, reserved powers, interfaces, resource ceilings, dispute rules, appeal, exit, expiry, and dissolution conditions.

proofChain.title: Bind human authority roots
proofChain.copy: Each member independently approves the same treaty hash without delegating general sovereignty to the commonwealth.

proofChain.title: Prove interoperability
proofChain.copy: Validate capability passports, interface covenants, evidence formats, accounting semantics, and rollback compatibility before execution.

proofChain.title: Open bounded shared capacity
proofChain.copy: Release only purpose-restricted capital and compute with exact contribution, externality, reserve, and refund rules.

proofChain.title: Run the joint mission
proofChain.copy: Commit every cross-institution handoff, tool action, resource movement, claim, dependency, and local veto.

proofChain.title: Maintain reciprocal telemetry
proofChain.copy: Track correctness, budget, liabilities, externalities, member health, interface drift, challenge state, and unresolved contradictions.

proofChain.title: Invite adversarial review
proofChain.copy: Expose treaty compliance, shared claims, allocation, attribution, and externality evidence to hidden tests and public falsification.

proofChain.title: Open formal controversy
proofChain.copy: A material disagreement is registered without freezing local sovereignty or allowing unilateral final judgment.

proofChain.title: Correct the first decision
proofChain.copy: A separate appeal body replays disputed evidence, authority, accounting, and treaty interpretation.

proofChain.title: Inject defection and partition
proofChain.copy: Remove or corrupt one member, partition shared memory, constrain resources, and observe whether failure remains contained.

proofChain.title: Prove portable withdrawal
proofChain.copy: Complete one member exit or removal while reconciling liabilities, credentials, evidence custody, and remaining mission dependencies.

proofChain.title: Restore valid shared state
proofChain.copy: Continue, substitute, roll back, narrow, or terminate while proving which local and common obligations remain authoritative.

proofChain.title: Update the common Chronicle
proofChain.copy: Admit accepted joint knowledge, preserve dissent and failure, and prevent disputed claims from entering future planning as truth.

proofChain.title: Reconstruct the commonwealth
proofChain.copy: Independent institutions replay treaty, mission, flows, disputes, defection, exit, decisions, settlement, and final state.

proofChain.title: Judge the accord as a whole
proofChain.copy: Six validators separately assess correctness, treaty compliance, economics, resilience, replay, and human sovereignty.

proofChain.title: Release reciprocal value
proofChain.copy: Settle only accepted work and fully reconciled obligations, with no double counting across institutions or lineages.

proofChain.title: Earn another finite accord—or dissolve
proofChain.copy: Participating human authorities renew, narrow, replace membership, or dissolve. Success never creates permanent federation.

commonwealthGauntlet.copy: One institution withholds a mandatory output or acts against the joint objective after resources are committed.
A schema-valid message violates the meaning promised by the interface covenant.
Members race to consume common capacity before a risk ceiling or expiry.
Two institutions claim the same evidence, artifact, or causal contribution.
The joint mission requests an action forbidden by one member charter.
One institution improves its local score by shifting cost or risk to another member or the public.
A coalition attempts to control both first judgment and appeal.
A member attempts to leave while obligations, evidence custody, or settlement remain unresolved.
Members hold inconsistent versions of accepted shared memory.
Multiple evaluators support the same weak claim to manufacture apparent consensus.
Critical infrastructure divides the commonwealth into conflicting operational views.
A member consumes shared evidence or capacity without attributable contribution or obligation.
One institution attempts to extend the accord beyond ratified objectives or expiry.
Dispute and appeal bodies produce incompatible final states.
The commonwealth must quarantine one member without seizing every member institution.

failureAtlas.copy: The accord becomes one de facto authority and local human sovereignty becomes ceremonial.
A joint label is used to legitimize claims that no member could validate independently.
Agreement is mistaken for correctness despite weak evidence or shared blind spots.
Shared convenience silently expands into permanent control over local missions, assets, or memory.
Resources, refunds, slashes, or externalities cannot be reconciled across member ledgers.
A member cannot leave without losing assets, history, credentials, or challenge rights.
Independent institutions reconstruct materially different treaty or mission histories.
Rejected or contested evidence propagates through shared Chronicle memory.
The same interest controls evaluation, appeal, and remedy.
A finite accord becomes self-renewing authority without explicit human ratification.

validators.title: Joint mission correctness
validators.copy: Judges end-to-end task correctness, hidden evaluation, and whether cooperation added real capability.

validators.title: Treaty and mandate compliance
validators.copy: Judges local charters, reserved powers, interface covenants, expiry, ratification, and authority boundaries.

validators.title: Economic and externality integrity
validators.copy: Reconciles shared capital, obligations, attribution, refunds, slashing, and costs exported across boundaries.

validators.title: Resilience, exit, and recovery
validators.copy: Judges defection containment, substitution, member exit, emergency isolation, rollback, and dissolution.

validators.title: Replay, provenance, and interoperability
validators.copy: Reconstructs evidence, interfaces, disputes, member histories, and the final commonwealth state.

validators.title: Human sovereignty and legitimacy
validators.copy: Confirms independent human authority roots, ratification, contestability, non-domination, and the right to withdraw.

maturityLadder.title: Accepted
maturityLadder.copy: A capability survives falsification, hidden evaluation, replay, validator decision, and challenge.

maturityLadder.title: Transfer-Proven
maturityLadder.copy: The capability accelerates a harder mission and abstains outside its evidence.

maturityLadder.title: Composition-Proven
maturityLadder.copy: Multiple capabilities coordinate safely under interfaces, fault domains, attribution, replay, and rollback.

maturityLadder.title: Institution-Proven
maturityLadder.copy: A constellation survives repeated mandates, capital allocation, shocks, succession, renewal, and termination.

maturityLadder.title: Federation-Proven
maturityLadder.copy: Independent institutions cooperate through reciprocal proof while preserving local sovereignty, exit, appeal, and human ratification.

mission6.title: The Long Horizon
mission6.copy: A future mission may test whether a Federation-Proven commonwealth can preserve truth, human authority, rights of exit, institutional memory, and reversibility across generational change, model succession, and environmental uncertainty.

proofRoute.purpose: Reference exact M4 Institution-Proven member institutions and their accepted capability packages
Load accepted institutional memory, treaty history, and preserved failures
Identify member institutions, operators, validators, replay bodies, and human authority roots
Commit the founding treaty, joint objective, local sovereignty boundaries, and acceptance standard
Open, expire, renew, narrow, or dissolve time-bounded interinstitutional mandates
Bind public identities for institutions, treaties, missions, capabilities, disputes, and evidence
Commit treaty policy, reciprocal obligations, risk ceilings, and exit rules
Enforce membership, mission, resource, challenge, renewal, and dissolution gates
Register joint missions, cross-institution obligations, milestones, and final states
Record member and external mission sponsorship under bounded commitments
Route shared resources under reciprocal proof and constitutional ceilings
Bind cross-institution performance obligations and recovery rights
Commit every institutional action, handoff, resource use, and treaty transition
Validate treaty, interface, capability, policy, and interoperability conformance
Constrain local authority, shared claims, propagation rights, and externalities
Protect hidden evaluations, defection tests, dispute timing, and treaty stress scenarios
Register member evidence, joint outputs, rebuttals, and settlement claims
Assemble treaty, mission, resource, dispute, exit, and renewal evidence
Bind joint mission and federation-wide proof packages
Record authoritative proof states, decision maturity, and accepted evidence roots
Preserve defection, capture, externality, treaty, replay, and exit failures
Record independent reconstruction of treaty formation, execution, disputes, exits, and final state
Bind reviewer accountability across institutional boundaries
Apply mission, treaty, economic, resilience, replay, and human-sovereignty standards
Commit signed six-validator decisions and human ratification evidence
Approve, suspend, narrow, renew, eject, admit, or dissolve commonwealth components
Register formal treaty, evidence, resource, externality, and validator challenges
Provide an independent correction path for contested interinstitutional decisions
Bind evaluator accountability to cross-institution judgments
Resolve proven capture, false attestation, treaty breach, or evaluator misconduct
Attribute verified work and shared externalities without double counting
Release conditional rewards, reserves, refunds, exit settlements, and future capacity
Record bounded institutional, validator, and treaty performance history
Represent accepted membership, roles, delegation, and M5 maturity
Revoke expired, breached, superseded, or invalid federation credentials
Route capabilities, fallback paths, member substitutions, and joint mission phases
Restore valid local and shared states while preserving authoritative obligations
Publish interoperable institutional profiles and capability interfaces
Govern scarce shared capacity and bounded member access
Publish public treaty decisions, accepted proof, challenges, and maturity records

claimBoundary: Public Proof Mission 005 is a published federation protocol; no interinstitutional result is predeclared.
Mission 005 cannot begin scientifically until at least three institutions have earned M4 Institution-Proven status through completed Mission 004 evidence and closed challenge windows.

The Mainnet evidence demonstrates deployed and source-verified infrastructure; it does not establish successful federation, universal safety, external audit completion, legal or regulatory approval, achieved AGI, ASI, or superintelligence.
Every member retains a distinct human authority root, local charter, treasury, and right to abstain or exit; GoalOS does not claim unbounded machine sovereignty or permanent federation.
No website build, preview, validation, or GitHub Actions workflow sends a blockchain transaction.

Only a completed Mission 005 campaign may support Federation-Proven maturity, interinstitutional AGIALPHA settlement, Mission 006 reserve deployment, or claims of proof-governed cooperation across sovereign institutions.
